pattern: The total part count is found by searching for the smallest number of parts whose suffix overhead still leaves room for the whole message, then the parts are filled greedily under that fixed count.
hints: The suffix length depends on the total number of parts, which is the very thing you are choosing, so test candidate counts.
For a fixed candidate count b, each part loses characters to its '<a/b>' suffix; sum the available content capacity across all b parts and check it covers the message length.

Increase b until the available capacity first reaches the message length (and each part can hold at least one content char), then slice the message into those parts attaching the correct suffixes; if no b works return an empty list.

edge_inputs.description: many small parts of equal length
input: this is really a very awesome message

edge_inputs.description: limit too small for any suffix to fit, impossible
crossing the digit-count boundary at part ten

stress.description: random lowercase-and-space messages with varied limits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz '
    msg = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    limit = rng.randint(1, 40)
    return [msg, limit]
stress.note: small limits frequently produce the impossible (empty) case

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def splitMessage(self, message, limit):
        n = len(message)
        a = 1
        b = 1
        cap = 0
        while True:
            suffix_len = len(str(a)) + len(str(b)) + 3
            avail = limit - suffix_len
            if avail <= 0:
                b += 1
                if b > n:
                    return []
                a = 1
                cap = 0
                continue
            cap += avail
            if a == b:
                if cap >= n:
                    break
                b += 1
                if b > n:
                    return []
                a = 1
                cap = 0
            else:
                a += 1
        res = []
        pos = 0
        for i in range(1, b + 1):
            suffix = '<%d/%d>' % (i, b)
            take = limit - len(suffix)
            chunk = message[pos:pos + take]
            res.append(chunk + suffix)
            pos += len(chunk)
        return res
